title: Skopeo copy stuck
body:
Hello,

I have requirement to copy images from jfrog artifactory to Alicloud hosted container registry. when I do skopeo copy, it exactly stuck before copying last blob for each image. 

​09:34:10 Copying docker://common.repositories.cloud.sap/devops-mirror/gokulb574/k8s-login-go:0.0.1 -> docker://ali-registry.cn-shanghai.cr.aliyuncs.com/big/devops-mirror/gokulb574/k8s-login-go:0.0.1
09:34:11 Getting image source signatures
09:34:11 Copying blob sha256:8bc205f2387822e8e5728d3389efa0d619ab30efcd1ecae3a7a89d9da87a216f
09:34:11 Copying blob sha256:ad0480257cbf62b5ad290972d7652d22bfd8248a26e86f0af4968e84b37d2ec9
09:34:11 Copying blob sha256:2d81dd8cd74e8f65de7516d28e8fc0e15f7ab71ee282a33f048767e0a4c1d58c
09:34:11 Copying blob sha256:f18232174bc91741fdf3da96d85011092101a032a93a388b79e99e69c2d5c870
09:34:11 Copying blob sha256:ed9b0c777f723fc2aa21549cb0ed0e79e463f1e26e1181b5ccc36f207c1a320c
09:35:10 ERROR: Failed to copy image: devops-mirror/gokulb574/k8s-login-go:0.0.1. Skipping...

Can anyone help me understand where and what goes wrong? is it because of network issue.. or something else. because it happens same for all the images... none of the images are getting copied.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

> Failed to copy image: devops-mirror/gokulb574/k8s-login-go:0.0.1. Skipping...

That text is, AFAICS, not emitted by Skopeo in _any_ version. And I would expect Skopeo to actually output some error with a _reason_. Is this wrapped in some script that discards stderr, or something like that?

There _might_ be some more detail available with `--debug`; pipe through something like `|& cat` to avoid the interactive progress bars.

At the very least, it should show which operations were actually initiated and what is the last one.

In general, as far as the protocol goes, the last _blob_ is not special in any way; so my first guess is that the issue actually happens when creating the image structure, and that the destination registry is applying some sort of policy / scanning. But that’s a guess with nothing to support it, at this point.

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I’m afraid that doesn’t actually contain the reported error either.  The last PATCH line is the actual upload of the large data, but … then what? It didn’t report an error, there is nothing to suggest that the command crashed.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if more information becomes available.
